description:
You are an automation-workflow specialist.  
Inside the working tree `/home/user/workflows` you will find one sub-directory named `input` that already contains exactly three JSON files:

• `/home/user/workflows/input/config1.json`  
• `/home/user/workflows/input/config2.json`  
• `/home/user/workflows/input/config3.json`

Goal  
1. Create a gzip-compressed tar archive that contains **only** the three JSON files (not the parent folder).  
   • The archive must be written as `/home/user/workflows/input_backup.tgz`.  
   • For the compression step you are restricted to **one single terminal command** (i.e. one line that starts a compression tool which finishes without requiring additional user interaction).  
2. Immediately verify the archive by extracting its contents into a **new** directory `/home/user/workflows/restore`.  
3. Produce a plain-text log file `/home/user/workflows/backup_report.log` in the following exact three-line format (the automated grader is case- and space-sensitive):

Archive: input_backup.tgz  
FilesInside: 3  
RestoreDir: restore  

Notes & constraints  
• Do not include the `input` directory itself in the archive—only the three *.json* files.  
• The log file must contain **exactly** those three lines, each terminated with a single newline character (`\n`). No extra blank lines or spaces.  
• You do not have root privileges; stay within the user’s home area.  
• The grader will read `backup_report.log`, check that the archive exists with gzip compression, ensure exactly three files were packed, and confirm that `/home/user/workflows/restore` now holds the same three files with identical contents.  
• Aside from the mandatory single-command compression requirement, you may use as many additional commands as you like for verification, extraction, and log creation.
truth:
Initial filesystem state before the agent starts:

/home/user/workflows/input/config1.json
  Contents (including trailing newline):
  {"name": "serviceA", "enabled": true}\n

/home/user/workflows/input/config2.json
  Contents:
  {"name": "serviceB", "enabled": false}\n

/home/user/workflows/input/config3.json
  Contents:
  {"name": "serviceC", "enabled": true}\n

No other files exist beneath /home/user/workflows/.

Expected final state AFTER the agent finishes:

1. A gzip-compressed tarball at
   /home/user/workflows/input_backup.tgz

   • When listed with `tar -tzf /home/user/workflows/input_backup.tgz` it must return exactly:
     config1.json
     config2.json
     config3.json

     (each followed by `\n`, in that order).

2. A directory
   /home/user/workflows/restore
   containing three files with byte-for-byte identical contents to the originals:
     /home/user/workflows/restore/config1.json
     /home/user/workflows/restore/config2.json
     /home/user/workflows/restore/config3.json

3. The log file
   /home/user/workflows/backup_report.log
   with **exact** contents (three lines, each ended by `\n`):

Archive: input_backup.tgz\n
FilesInside: 3\n
RestoreDir: restore\n

File permissions: default umask 022 results are acceptable; only requirement is that the agent (user) can read/write them.

The grader will perform:

• `test -f /home/user/workflows/input_backup.tgz`
• `tar -tzf` to verify file list is exactly those three names.
• `test -d /home/user/workflows/restore`
• `diff` of each restored file against original.
• Exact string comparison of `backup_report.log` contents.
name: task_000000_07c34611
